name: Boleskine House, Loch Ness
significance: Site of the incomplete Abramelin working, considered the focal point of Crowley's magical system in the early 1900s.

name: Cairo, Egypt
significance: The Boulaq Museum and the apartment where the Book of the Law was received in 1904.

name: Abbey of Thelema, Cefalù
significance: Crowley's magical commune in Sicily (1920-1923), infamous for the death of Raoul Loveday.

name: Bou Saâda, Algeria
significance: Where Crowley and Victor Neuburg scried the Enochian Aethyrs (The Vision and the Voice) in 1909.
